title: pynewsreader
text:
Command Line Usage
# Show help
pnr --help

# Add a feed
pnr add_feed https://rss.cbc.ca/lineup/world.xml

# Update feed(s)
pnr update

# Show entries
pnr show --limit 10

# Search entries
pnr search canada

title: pynewsreader
text:
Web Application
The main interface for viewing the RSS feeds is the Flask web application, which can be lauched from the command line:
pnr-app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 5000
# Or
python3 -m pynewsreader.app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 5000

title: pynewsreader
text:
Python Usage
Import and initialize pnr object

from pynewsreader.core import Feed, PyNewsReader

pnr = PyNewsReader()


pnr.feeds()

[Feeds(url='https://openai.com/blog/rss.xml', name='OpenAI Blog'),
 Feeds(url='https://ricochet.media/en/feed', name='Richochet Media'),

Update feeds

pnr.update()

Marking entry as important: Harold the Mortgage Closer accused of charging ‘vulnerable’ people interest rates up to
76 per cent



List entries

pnr.show(limit=1)

╭────────────────────────────────────────── Toronto Star | Top Stories ───────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ Title: Taylor Swift inspires Justin Trudeau to show off his dances moves at Toronto concert                     │
│ Date: 2024-11-23                                                                                                │
│                                                                                                                 │
│ The Prime Minister's Office confirmed that Trudeau and his family attended the fifth of Swift's six concerts    │
│ Friday night at the Rogers Centre.                                                                              │
│                                                                                                                 │
╰─ https://www.thestar.com/entertainment/music/taylor-swift-inspires-justin-trudeau-to-show-off-his-dances-moves-─╯






Search entries

pnr.search("inflation", limit=1)

╭──────────────────────────────────────────────── CBC | Business ─────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ Title: Inflation ticked up to 2% in October after previous month's steep drop in gas prices                     │
│ Date: 2024-11-19                                                                                                │
│                                                                                                                 │
│ Canada's inflation rate ticked up to two per cent in October, Statistics Canada said on Tuesday. The consumer   │
│ price index had previously hit 1.6 per cent in September, thanks to a steep decline in gas prices.              │
│                                                                                                                 │
╰─────────────────── https://www.cbc.ca/news/business/inflation-october-2024-1.7387125?cmp=rss ───────────────────╯






Blacklist

for i in [
    "Musk",
    "Apple",
    "Bezos",
    "Google",
    "Samsung",
    "iPhone",
    "iPad",
    "guardian.com/sport/",
    "guardian.com/football/",
    "thestar.com/sports",
    "cbc.ca/sports"
]:
    pnr.blacklist_add(i)

Whitelist

# Automatically marks as "Important"
for i in ["interest rate", "Bank of Canada", "housing market"]:
    pnr.whitelist_add(i)

title: core
text:
Add Feeds

# With names
for i in [
    Feed(url="https://ricochet.media/en/feed", name="Richochet Media"),
    Feed(url="https://thetyee.ca/rss2.xml", name="The Tyee"),
]:
    r.add_feed(i)

# Use the default name from the feed
for i in [
    "https://www.theguardian.com/science/rss",
    "https://www.theguardian.com/world/canada/rss",
]:
    r.add_feed(Feed(i))

title: core
text:
Display Entries
read = None shows all entries
read = False shows only unread entries
read = True shows only read entries

r.show(limit=5, read=None, mark_as_read=False)

title: core
text:
Tag Entry

test = list(r._reader.get_entries())


r._reader.set_tag(test[0], "foobar")


list(r._reader.get_tags(test[0]))


# We added a method to just return the tag key:
r._get_tags(test[0])

title: core
text:
Remove tag from entry

r._reader.delete_tag(test[0], "foobar")


list(r._get_tags(test[0]))

title: core
text:
Mark as Important/Unimportant

r._mark_important(test[0].feed_url, test[0].id)


r._mark_unimportant(test[0].feed_url, test[0].id)

title: core
text:
Mark as Read / Unread

r._reader.mark_entry_as_read(test[0])
r._reader.mark_entry_as_unread(test[0])

title: core
text:
Automatically mark entries as read
When updating feeds, entries with titles matching these strings will automatically be marked as read

for i in [
    "Musk",
    "Apple",
    "Bezos",
    "Google",
    "Samsung",
    "iPhone",
    "iPad",
    "guardian.com/sport/",
    "guardian.com/football/",
    "thestar.com/sports",
]:
    r.blacklist_add(i)


r.blacklist_show()


# r.update()

title: core
text:
Automatically mark entries as important

for i in ["interest rate", "Bank of Canada", "housing market"]:
    r.whitelist_add(i)


r.whitelist_show()


# r.update()

title: core
text:
Low level access to data

entries = r._get_entries(limit=1)
entry = [i for i in entries][0]
entry
